headlines.title: Everman's Departure One of Biggest Railroad Events
headlines.subtitle: T. & P. Man Who Goes to Cott on Belt Came to Texas When He Was Only Twenty Years Old.
headlines.fullText:
July 1, .being the beginning of the fiscal year for the railroads, never passes without seeing some Important change in Texas railroad circles. The feature change this year concerns John W. Everman and J. E. Taussig .. wenn superintendent of fe Tezas & Pacific into the berth of vice presi- dent and general manager of the St. Louis-Southwestern. Taussig succeeds Everman.
Almost a Native.
Everman has been in Texas for so many years that he is claimed an a native son, and the whole state is tak- ing interest in his promotion, John W. Everman was born Feb. 1. 1861, and was graduated by De bonne schools of Philadelphia In 1876. Dur- ing Isit and 1878 he was engaged In returned to the United States and went almost directly to Philadelphia. Then he began his railroad career mere boy acting as clerk in the ticket ruad. In his early work he showed marked ability to get alcal and cum more than his Job Calles turban 1881 he resigned his Philadelphia Job and came to Texas, Kalns lo shall. From one clerk's job to another Everman went Iverman went, all the time attending marked his career in these later years.
Promotions Besin.
afterward he was made a chief clerk, then yatene to a superintendent, all the time, how- ever, with the Texas & Pacific. The two years that followed May 19, 1892, a general superintend- ent, and two years after that became assistant general manager. For the past three years Everman bezauberintendent has been general superintendent of the Texas & Pacific.

headlines.title: ASSAULT TO MU .. DER COMPLAINT IS FILED
headlines.subtitle: Man Stabbed in Trouble Near Kennedale in Serious Condition.
headlines.fullText: Frank P. Whitaker, known as "Lou- istana Shorty," was arrested near Ken- nedale Saturday by Deputy Sheriff Buck Williams, charged with stabbing Jim Bolon Friday, and was lodged in the county Jail. Information chalets Assault to murder was filed him in Justice samen com. Whitaker apparently haj made no hent to cacape, He left the pipe line und obtained work Saturday morning with a road gang just this side of Ken- ums. When first arrested he claimed that his name was Johnson, but later he admitted that he was the man wanted. Late Saturday It was stated at the University Hospital, where Solon had been taken, that his condition was very serious. It was found that the knife penetrated his liver and he was hav- ing hemorrhages late Saturday.

headlines.title: $22,000,000 GAIN PREDICTED FOR U. S.
headlines.subtitle: House Leader Estimates Revenues Under New Tariff Bill at $292,000,000.
headlines.fullText: WASHINGTON, June 27 .- Democrat- le Leader Underwood told the house today the tariff law would produce 3292,000,000 for the year, $22,000,000 more than the estimatea. The Income tax this year, he said, would produce $86.000,000, $10,000.000 less than the estimates, because in- comes were not taxed for the full year. He urged that the treasury's million dollar fund for collecting the tax Increased to a million and a half.

headlines.title: MAN TURNING BLUE
headlines.subtitle: Springfield Citizen Sues Deeter, Al. leging Treatment Caused Trouble
headlines.fullText: SEHRGELD Mo. June 21-Win- um st. Thomas, a farmer living eight Iam a Anne springfield, is turning NuThe skin of his face, neck and body has asumed a peculiar bluth tingE. In an action which is being tried In the circuit court here he is walne Dr. W McFarland Brown for 118. damages for mai practice in giving him repeated masal douches of nitrate of silver with water as a treatratar his throat. He alleges the medicine is responsible for his color.

Manufacturing Optician. DANGER IN READY-MADE CLASSES. By "ready-made" glasses we mean those sold over z counter like groceries. Such glasses are apt to do more harm than good be- cause they are generally too large or too small, to say nothing of the focal power being too weak or too strong. Eyes differ. They do not run in sizes like shoes. Hence, it must be determined what your eyes need before the need can be supplied. It is better to have your eyes examined and get the right glasses in the first place than to waste money by experimenting with glasses never intended to help your eyes-just made to sell. Let tis confer with you on this question.
